_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 1 derived from live edition-2 cards via scripts/cards-to-stage1.mjs. Stage 3 (Codex) verification scored FAS=74 with 11 verified claims. Headline framing 'twice in a month' replaced with 'three times in five months' to reflect the actual 6 Dec 2025 / 5 Jan 2026 / 20 Apr 2026 timing. Reframe and view cards softened to drop OVERCLAIM cardinal-sin flags on political-causation framing. ACCCIM 700,000-800,000 SME count removed per Stage 3 UNVERIFIED flag (SAMENTA 200,000 retained as verified). Phase 1-2 compliance 90%+, 820m invoices, RM3.5B undeclared income surfaced, RM484m / 5,800 taxpayer flag — all VERIFIED per Stage 3, retained.

headline: E-invoice mandate softened three times in five months after Sabah loss
context: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election loss, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption from RM500K to RM1m. The RM1m-5m transition was extended on 5 January 2026 and again on 20 April 2026. Phase 1-2 compliance was over 90%.
revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING_FRAMING — Headline 'walked back twice in a month' → 'softened three times in five months' (6 Dec 2025 + 5 Jan 2026 + 20 Apr 2026 — three events spanning five months).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED_DETAIL — ACCCIM 700,000-800,000 SME count dropped from hook sub; only SAMENTA 200,000 (verified) retained.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 expected): hook sub 'Reporting linked the timing to PH's urban vote losses in Sabah' was OVERCLAIM per Stage 3 — softened to 'The announcement came at a Sabah-Madani engagement days after the election' (factual rather than causation-attributing).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING_FRAMING — Fact 2 sub 'The range reflects definition, not measurement' → 'The spread reflects both definition and methodology' per Stage 3 correction.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — Reframe 'the rule moves where political pressure shows' softened to 'the rule moves on a political clock' (still editorially sharp but less attributively causal).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): rhetorical-not-supported — View card 'the rule walks back faster than the shadow economy shrinks' replaced with 'three softenings in five months set a different pattern' (concrete observation rather than rhetorical comparison).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 6 Dec 2025 exemption raise + Sabah election timing; 5 Jan 2026 transition extension; 20 Apr 2026 second extension; Phase 1-2 compliance over 90%; 820.5m e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers; 21% shadow economy / RM330B government estimate; Khazanah 4-7.8% / World Economics 26.6%; LHDN July 2025 5,800 taxpayer flag for RM484m; RM3.5B undeclared income surfaced by April 2026.
